documentation.comment: Homepage set to docs.vizia.dev in the GitHub repo metadata; an in-progress book lives at book.vizia.dev

maintenance-status.comment: 0.4.0 released 2026-04-23; 1,843 commits on main; repo pushed to same day as research

maturity.comment: Still pre-1.0 (0.4.0); breaking changes between 0.3 and 0.4 (styling system overhaul per nih_plug_vizia notes)

corporate-backing.comment: Primary maintainer is Dr George Atkinson (geom3trik); no corporate sponsor identified

implementation.comment: Framework code is pure Rust with no native GUI toolkit wrapping. Rendering goes through skia-safe (Rust binding to Google's C++ Skia library) — classified as pure-rust here because Skia is a rendering primitive, consistent with how wgpu-based frameworks are classified

rendering-mode.comment: Views are ECS entities with persistent state; bindings update the retained tree on state change

reactivity-model.comment: Bindings observe model state and rebuild affected views (signals-like). Underneath, widgets are mutated in an ECS (retained-tree)

binding-target.comment: Pure Rust; no native GUI toolkit wrapped

thread-model.comment: UI runs on the main thread; cross-thread communication via Application::get_proxy() returning a ContextProxy

renderer.comment: Skia canvas drawn via an OpenGL context created by winit. Platform deps pull in skia-safe with metal + GL on macOS and x11/wayland + GL on Linux

gpu-acceleration.comment: Skia canvas is GPU-backed via OpenGL (and Metal on macOS via skia-safe features)

hidpi-support.comment: Skia + winit combination handles HiDPI scaling; no specific issue reported

vector-rendering.comment: Skia provides vector primitives; Vizia ships 4,250+ SVG Tabler Icons and has an svg view

custom-shaders.comment: Not advertised as a feature; Skia supports SkSL but no documentation shows Vizia exposing shader injection to apps

embedded-custom-rendering.comment: No documented API for embedding a custom wgpu/OpenGL viewport inside a Vizia view

linux-x11.comment: x11 is a default feature flag

linux-wayland.comment: wayland is a default feature flag

ios.comment: Self-described as a "declarative desktop GUI framework"; no mobile targets listed

web-wasm.comment: No WASM target documented; framework relies on native OpenGL + Skia

embedded-no-std.comment: Uses std, async, Skia — not suitable for no_std

authoring-style.comment: README: "Write GUI code in a declarative way in pure Rust" — no DSL or RSX macros; views are composed via Rust builder-style calls

hot-reload.comment: Hot-reloadable CSS stylesheets (examples/stylesheet.rs); Rust view code itself still requires recompile

layout-system.comment: Layout engine is morphorm (flex-inspired); examples include grid.rs, hstack/vstack/zstack

styling-approach.comment: Uses cssparser + selectors crates; real CSS stylesheets with hot reload

theme-switching.comment: Ships light and dark built-in themes; stylesheet hot reload enables runtime theme switching

accessibility.comment: accesskit is a default feature flag in 0.3+

internationalization.comment: Uses fluent-bundle for localization; examples/l10n.rs shows usage

widget-breadth.comment: 30+ built-in widgets including table, virtual_table, virtual_list, markdown, menu/menubar, combobox, calendar, knob, xypad — but no tree/outline widget and no charting. Solidly in "most common + a few advanced" territory

data-grid-table.comment: Both Table (variable row heights) and VirtualTable (virtualized for large datasets) are first-party views

rich-text-markdown.comment: markdown feature flag enabled by default; Markdown view plus rich_text.rs example

tree-outline.comment: No Tree/TreeView widget in the views module; users would need to compose one

native-menus.comment: MenuBar and Menu views exist but are drawn by Vizia via Skia, not using the OS-native menu bar

file-dialogs.comment: save_dialog.rs example; typically wired via the rfd crate (Vizia doesn't ship its own native dialog code)

custom-widgets.comment: examples/custom_view.rs demonstrates the View trait; authoring custom widgets is documented and idiomatic

async-runtime-compatible.comment: Framework is runtime-agnostic via Application::get_proxy() (ContextProxy allows sending events from any thread/runtime), but no specific runtime is officially blessed in the docs. Tokio appears in the workspace Cargo.toml but that's internal, not a user-facing guarantee

notifications.comment: No built-in OS notification integration documented

clipboard.comment: clipboard is a default feature flag

drag-and-drop.comment: examples/dragdrop.rs demonstrates intra-app drag-and-drop; DropData::File handles OS-level file drops onto the app

multi-window.comment: Window::new() with an on_close hook; example works with the winit backend (panics under the baseview/audio-plugin backend)

binary-size.comment: Not measured in this pass; Skia-based frameworks are typically in the moderate-to-large range, but I lack a verified measurement

idle-redraw.comment: README: "further optimizations to only draw what is necessary"; retained-mode with binding-driven redraw

dependency-count.comment: Not measured via cargo tree in this pass; Skia + winit + accesskit + fluent + cssparser suggest at least moderate, likely heavy, but unverified

documentation-quality.comment: Has docs.rs API + an in-progress book + 50+ examples + widget gallery. Book is described as a tutorial/reference but coverage of advanced topics appears incomplete

api-ergonomics.comment: Idiomatic Rust builder patterns, Lens/Data-style derive macros for bindings, no lifetime-heavy view signatures

examples-count.comment: 40+ view examples + 14 top-level examples + gallery + widget_gallery + 7GUIs + todo = well over 20

interactive-playground.comment: No online playground; widget gallery is a desktop example, not web-hosted

ide-tooling.comment: No framework-specific LSP/inspector; since views are plain Rust and styles are plain CSS, generic tooling (rust-analyzer, CSS language servers) covers the editor story

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads of the top-level vizia crate as of 2026-04-24; recent_downloads=366. Modest for an older project — plugin authors often depend on nih_plug_vizia rather than vizia directly

notable-production-users.value: Audio plugins built with nih-plug + vizia-plug (various independent VST3/CLAP plugins); no flagship shipping application widely known
notable-production-users.comment: nih-plug's supported GUI frameworks include Vizia alongside egui and iced; audio-plugin niche is where Vizia sees most production use

community-size.comment: Active Discord and 100 forks on the repo; fewer than large/medium chat thresholds

ecosystem-crates.comment: vizia-plug for audio plugins, nih_plug_vizia integration; limited third-party themes/widget crates
